gaps: No test covers detection of active call state on Voyager 4245 Office (Morpheus) devices via the Poly Studio Mobile iOS app.
No test verifies that the app correctly identifies the deckardId for Morpheus devices to enable call-aware features.
No test covers the blocking of DFU (firmware update) during an active call on Morpheus devices.
No test covers the blocking of ANC/Transparency Mode changes during an active call on Morpheus devices.
No test covers call-aware alert behavior (e.g., UI indicators, toasts, or banners) when an active call is detected on a Morpheus device.
No test validates that unsupported or missing deckardId causes failure in call state detection, which is the root cause of this bug.

generated_testcases.title: TC-4245-CALL-001: Verify iOS app detects active call state on Voyager 4245 Office (Morpheus) via correct deckardId
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app on iOS device.
Tap the connected Voyager 4245 Office (Morpheus) device name from the device list.
Navigate to Device Info or About screen and verify the device is recognized as 'Voyager 4245 Office' (confirm deckardId is supported).
Return to the main device screen.
Place an active outgoing call from the iOS device (e.g., dial a test number).
Wait for the call to connect (caller hears ringing/connected).
Observe the Poly Studio Mobile app UI for any call-aware indicators (e.g., banner, toast, status text, or icon indicating 'On a call' or 'Call active').
While the call is active, navigate to Audio Settings → Transparency Mode.
Attempt to adjust the Surround level slider (e.g., set to 50).
Attempt to adjust the Free level slider (e.g., set to 50).
Navigate to Audio Settings → ANC Level and attempt to change the ANC level.
Navigate to Device Settings → Firmware Update and attempt to start a DFU.
generated_testcases.expected: The Poly Studio Mobile app correctly detects the active call state on the Voyager 4245 Office (Morpheus) device. A call-aware indicator (banner, toast, or status text) is displayed. ANC/Transparency Mode changes are blocked or disabled during the active call. DFU (firmware update) is blocked or shows a warning that a call is in progress. The app does not silently fail to detect the call state.

generated_testcases.title: TC-4245-CALL-002: Verify deckardId detection for Morpheus device enables call-aware features
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app on iOS device.
Connect to a Voyager 4245 Office (Morpheus) headset.
Navigate to Device → About / Device Information.
Locate the field displaying the device model or deckardId (e.g., 'Model: Voyager 4245 Office' or 'deckardId: ...').
Take a screenshot or note the displayed identifier.
Place an active call from the iOS device.
While on the call, navigate to the main device screen in the app.
Verify that the app displays a call-active state indicator (e.g., 'On a call' badge, phone icon, or toast notification).
End the call.
Verify the call-active indicator disappears and normal settings control is restored.
generated_testcases.expected: The app correctly identifies the Voyager 4245 Office (Morpheus) device via its deckardId. When a call is active, the app shows a call-aware indicator. When the call ends, the indicator is removed and full settings control is restored. This confirms the deckardId is properly mapped to enable call-aware features.

generated_testcases.title: TC-4245-CALL-003: Verify DFU (firmware update) is blocked during an active call on Morpheus device
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app on iOS device.
Connect to a Voyager 4245 Office (Morpheus) headset.
Place an active outgoing call from the iOS device and wait for connection.
While the call is active, navigate to Device Settings → Firmware Update.
Tap 'Check for Updates' or 'Update Now' (if an update is available).
Observe the app response: look for a blocking dialog, toast, or disabled button.
If a dialog appears, read the message text and tap OK/Dismiss.
End the call.
Immediately navigate back to Firmware Update and attempt to start the update again.
generated_testcases.expected: During the active call, the DFU (firmware update) process is blocked. The app displays a clear message such as 'Cannot update during a call' or the update button is disabled/greyed out. After the call ends, the DFU option becomes available again and the update can proceed normally.

generated_testcases.title: TC-4245-CALL-004: Verify ANC/Transparency Mode changes are blocked during an active call on Morpheus device
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app on iOS device.
Connect to a Voyager 4245 Office (Morpheus) headset.
Note the current ANC level and Transparency Mode settings (Surround and Free levels).
Place an active outgoing call from the iOS device and wait for connection.
While on the call, navigate to Audio Settings → ANC Level.
Attempt to change the ANC level (e.g., slide from current to a different value).
Navigate to Audio Settings → Transparency Mode → Environment.
Attempt to adjust the Surround level slider to a new value (e.g., 75).
Navigate to Transparency Mode → Speech.
Attempt to adjust the Free level slider to a new value (e.g., 40).
End the call.
Immediately navigate back to Audio Settings → ANC Level and check the value.
Navigate to Transparency Mode → Environment and check the Surround level.
Navigate to Transparency Mode → Speech and check the Free level.
generated_testcases.expected: During the active call, all ANC level changes and Transparency Mode slider adjustments (Surround and Free) are blocked or disabled. The sliders may appear greyed out, or changes are rejected. After the call ends, the settings return to their original values (before the call), confirming that no changes were applied during the call.

generated_testcases.title: TC-4245-CALL-005: Verify call-aware alert UI (banner/toast) appears when active call is detected on Morpheus device
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app on iOS device.
Connect to a Voyager 4245 Office (Morpheus) headset.
Ensure the app is in the foreground on the device screen.
Place an active outgoing call from the iOS device.
Observe the Poly Studio Mobile app UI for 10 seconds while the call connects.
Note any visual indicators: banner at top, toast popup, status bar change, or icon change on the device card.
While the call is active, navigate to different screens in the app (Audio Settings, Device Settings, Home).
Verify the call-aware indicator persists across screen navigation.
End the call.
Observe the app UI for 5 seconds after the call ends.
generated_testcases.expected: When the call becomes active, the Poly Studio Mobile app displays a clear call-aware alert UI element (e.g., a banner saying 'On a call', a toast notification, or a persistent status badge). This indicator remains visible as the user navigates between screens. When the call ends, the indicator disappears within a few seconds, confirming the app correctly detects the call state transition.

generated_testcases.title: TC-4245-CALL-006: Verify unsupported/missing deckardId causes call state detection failure (negative test)
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app on iOS device.
Connect to a Voyager 4245 Office (Morpheus) headset.
Navigate to Device → About / Device Information and record the deckardId or model identifier.
Simulate an unsupported deckardId scenario: either by using a test build where the deckardId is removed from the supported list, or by connecting a device with an unknown/unsupported deckardId.
Place an active outgoing call from the iOS device.
While on the call, navigate to Audio Settings → ANC Level and attempt to change it.
Navigate to Audio Settings → Transparency Mode → Environment and attempt to adjust the Surround level.
Navigate to Device Settings → Firmware Update and attempt to start a DFU.
Observe whether the app shows any call-aware indicator (banner/toast).
End the call.
generated_testcases.expected: When the deckardId is unsupported or missing from the app's device mapping, the app fails to detect the active call state. No call-aware indicator (banner/toast) is displayed. ANC level changes and Transparency Mode adjustments are incorrectly allowed during the call. DFU (firmware update) is also incorrectly allowed to proceed. This confirms that the missing/unsupported deckardId is the root cause of the call state detection failure.

generated_testcases.title: TC-4245-CALL-007: Verify call state detection on Morpheus device after app force-kill and relaunch during active call
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app on iOS device.
Connect to a Voyager 4245 Office (Morpheus) headset.
Place an active outgoing call from the iOS device and wait for connection.
While the call is active, force-kill the Poly Studio Mobile app (swipe up from app switcher).
Wait 5 seconds.
Re-launch the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Observe the app's home screen for any call-aware indicator.
Navigate to Audio Settings → ANC Level and attempt to change it.
Navigate to Audio Settings → Transparency Mode → Environment and attempt to adjust the Surround level.
Navigate to Device Settings → Firmware Update and attempt to start a DFU.
End the call.
Observe the app UI after the call ends.
generated_testcases.expected: After force-kill and relaunch during an active call, the Poly Studio Mobile app correctly detects the ongoing call state. A call-aware indicator (banner/toast) is displayed. ANC/Transparency Mode changes are blocked. DFU is blocked. After the call ends, the indicator disappears and settings control is restored.

generated_testcases.title: TC-4245-CALL-008: Verify call state detection on Morpheus device after Bluetooth disconnect/reconnect during active call
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app on iOS device.
Connect to a Voyager 4245 Office (Morpheus) headset.
Place an active outgoing call from the iOS device and wait for connection.
While the call is active, disconnect the headset via iOS Bluetooth settings (tap 'Disconnect' or turn off Bluetooth).
Wait 5 seconds.
Reconnect the headset via iOS Bluetooth settings or by powering the headset off and on.
Wait for the Poly Studio Mobile app to re-establish connection (observe the device card appearing).
Observe whether the app shows a call-aware indicator.
Navigate to Audio Settings → ANC Level and attempt to change it.
Navigate to Audio Settings → Transparency Mode → Environment and attempt to adjust the Surround level.
Navigate to Device Settings → Firmware Update and attempt to start a DFU.
End the call.
generated_testcases.expected: After Bluetooth disconnect and reconnect during an active call, the Poly Studio Mobile app correctly detects the ongoing call state. A call-aware indicator is displayed. ANC/Transparency Mode changes are blocked. DFU is blocked. The app does not lose track of the call state due to the Bluetooth reconnection.

generated_testcases.title: TC-4245-CALL-009: Verify call state detection on Morpheus device when receiving an incoming call
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app on iOS device.
Connect to a Voyager 4245 Office (Morpheus) headset.
From a second phone, place a call to the iOS device.
Answer the incoming call on the iOS device.
Observe the Poly Studio Mobile app UI for a call-aware indicator.
While on the call, navigate to Audio Settings → ANC Level and attempt to change it.
Navigate to Audio Settings → Transparency Mode → Environment and attempt to adjust the Surround level.
Navigate to Device Settings → Firmware Update and attempt to start a DFU.
End the call.
Observe the app UI after the call ends.
generated_testcases.expected: When an incoming call is answered on the Morpheus device, the Poly Studio Mobile app correctly detects the active call state. A call-aware indicator is displayed. ANC/Transparency Mode changes are blocked. DFU is blocked. After the call ends, the indicator disappears and settings control is restored.

generated_testcases.title: TC-4245-CALL-010: Verify call state detection on Morpheus device during call waiting/switching between two calls
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app on iOS device.
Connect to a Voyager 4245 Office (Morpheus) headset.
Place Call A from the iOS device to a first test number and wait for connection.
While on Call A, place Call B on hold and dial a second test number (call waiting).
Switch between Call A and Call B using the iOS call UI.
Observe the Poly Studio Mobile app UI during each call switch for the call-aware indicator.
While on Call B, navigate to Audio Settings → ANC Level and attempt to change it.
Navigate to Audio Settings → Transparency Mode → Environment and attempt to adjust the Surround level.
Navigate to Device Settings → Firmware Update and attempt to start a DFU.
End both calls.
Observe the app UI after all calls end.
generated_testcases.expected: The Poly Studio Mobile app correctly detects the active call state throughout call waiting and switching scenarios. The call-aware indicator persists across call switches. ANC/Transparency Mode changes are blocked during all active calls. DFU is blocked. After all calls end, the indicator disappears and settings control is restored.
